The OLAF Report 2025

In 2025, the European Anti-Fraud Office (OLAF) concluded 264 investigations and recommended the recovery of EUR 426.8 million to the EU budget.

Case summaries

Volkswagen AG — Diesel emissions subsidy fraud

Year concluded: 2024 | Status: Recommendation issued

OLAF investigated allegations that Volkswagen AG received EU research subsidies under false pretences related to clean-diesel technology. OLAF recommended the recovery of EUR 31.2 million and referred the case to national prosecutors in Germany.

BP plc — North Sea decommissioning fund misuse

Year concluded: 2023 | Status: Closed with financial recommendation

OLAF investigated BP plc over alleged misuse of EU decommissioning funds. The investigation resulted in a recommended recovery of EUR 8.7 million.

Carrefour SA — Agricultural subsidy irregularities

Year concluded: 2024 | Status: Ongoing investigation

OLAF opened an investigation into Carrefour SA regarding CAP (Common Agricultural Policy) subsidy claims in France. No financial recommendation has been issued at this time.

Lufthansa AG — Aviation safety research grant fraud

Year concluded: 2022 | Status: Closed, recommendation accepted

OLAF investigated Deutsche Lufthansa AG over Horizon 2020 grant misuse. EUR 4.1 million recovery recommended; case closed in 2022 after national-court settlement.

Nestle SA — EU food-safety research grant

Year concluded: 2023 | Status: Closed, no financial action

OLAF reviewed a Horizon Europe grant administered by Nestle SA. The investigation closed without a financial recommendation.
